citations.description: Search for `gold_set` across src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ returns no hits for any of the three files; only other specs (container, g7, finder, etc.) appear.

findings.criterion: Each of the three specs has a non-empty `gold_set`; each entry has an `input` and a `label` (`finding`|`pass`).
findings.detail: None of the three target files contain a `gold_set` key. A repository-wide search for `gold_set` in eval_specs/ returned no matches for `plan-review-asserted-capability.eval.yaml`, `plan-review-decomp-shape.eval.yaml`, or `plan-review-necessity.eval.yaml`. Reading all three files in full confirms no `gold_set` section is present in any of them.

findings.criterion: Each gold_set is balanced (>=1 `finding` and >=1 `pass` gold label), faithful to that spec's dataset.
findings.detail: Because no `gold_set` section exists in any of the three files, this balance requirement cannot be satisfied.

findings.criterion: `validate_eval_spec(spec, strict=True)` returns no errors for all dataset-bearing packaged specs.
findings.detail: The validator's `validate_dataset_and_gold` function (eval.py line 265-267) checks `gold = spec.get('gold_set')` and appends `"strict: eval spec needs a non-empty gold_set (judge kappa alignment)"` if it is absent or empty. Since none of the three files define a `gold_set`, `validate_eval_spec(..., strict=True)` will return this error for each of the three specs.

findings.criterion: The offline eval-discipline test suite passes; the `Prompt Eval` action is green.
findings.detail: The offline test suite includes tests that load all packaged plan-review specs and call `validate_eval_spec(spec, strict=True)` (test_plan_review_evals.py). The three advisory specs are not in the PLAN_REVIEW_SPECS list in that file, but `test_new_eval_specs.py` and other discipline tests iterate packaged specs. More directly: the three specs lack `gold_set`, so the CI gate (`validate_eval_spec(spec, strict=True)`) would still fail on any test that covers them. The root defect (missing `gold_set`) is unresolved, so the prompt-eval action cannot be green.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
